activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: You should have went to bed earlier.

evaluator.answer: You should have gone to bed earlier

activities.explanation: Después de «have» usamos el participio «gone», no el pasado «went».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: You shouldn't have to shared the private link.

evaluator.answer: You shouldn't have shared the private link

activities.explanation: La estructura es «shouldn't have + participio», sin «to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: We could had taken the direct train.

evaluator.answer: We could have taken the direct train

activities.explanation: Después del modal usamos «have», no «had».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: I should accepted their offer when I had the chance.

evaluator.answer: I should have accepted their offer when I had the chance

activities.explanation: Falta «have» entre el modal y el participio.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: You should not have drove so fast in the rain.

evaluator.answer: You should not have driven so fast in the rain

activities.explanation: El participio de «drive» es «driven».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: We could have use the side entrance.

evaluator.answer: We could have used the side entrance

activities.explanation: Después de «have» necesitamos el participio «used».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: You needn't have to printed every page.

evaluator.answer: You needn't have printed every page

activities.explanation: «Needn't have» va directamente seguido del participio «printed».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: I didn't needed to call because the issue was already fixed.

evaluator.answer: I didn't need to call because the issue was already fixed

activities.explanation: Tras «didn't» usamos el infinitivo «need».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: The hotel provided towels, so I mustn't have brought one.

evaluator.answer: The hotel provided towels, so I needn't have brought one

activities.explanation: La acción se realizó pero era innecesaria; corresponde «needn't have».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: The warning was clear; you needn't have ignored it.

evaluator.answer: The warning was clear; you shouldn't have ignored it

activities.explanation: Ignorarla fue un error, no una acción innecesaria; usamos «shouldn't have».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: There was another route, so we should have taken it, but we didn't know about it.

evaluator.answer: There was another route, so we could have taken it, but we didn't know about it

activities.explanation: El contexto destaca una alternativa disponible, no una obligación moral: «could have taken».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: You should have not posted the address publicly.

evaluator.answer: You should not have posted the address publicly

activities.explanation: La posición natural de la negación es «should not have posted».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: We needn't have took a taxi; the station was nearby.

evaluator.answer: We needn't have taken a taxi; the station was nearby

activities.explanation: El participio de «take» es «taken».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: The backup existed; we could restored the folder immediately.

evaluator.answer: The backup existed; we could have restored the folder immediately

activities.explanation: La oportunidad pasada necesita «could have restored».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: I regret dismissing the idea; I could have considered it more carefully.

evaluator.answer: I regret dismissing the idea; I should have considered it more carefully

activities.explanation: El arrepentimiento evalúa lo recomendable, por lo que «should have» es más preciso.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: We didn't need to have printed the report, but we printed all 200 pages.

evaluator.answer: We needn't have printed the report, but we printed all 200 pages

activities.explanation: Como la acción sí ocurrió y fue innecesaria, la forma precisa es «needn't have printed».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: You could have to asked for a lower price.

evaluator.answer: You could have asked for a lower price

activities.explanation: La estructura es «could have + participio», sin «to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: You shouldn't had changed the database without a backup.

evaluator.answer: You shouldn't have changed the database without a backup

activities.explanation: Después de «shouldn't» usamos «have», no «had».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: The fix was remote, so we shouldn't have sent the device across the country.

evaluator.answer: The fix was remote, so we needn't have sent the device across the country

activities.explanation: El envío no era necesario; «needn't have» expresa mejor ese significado que una crítica.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: I should have readed the explanation before complaining.

evaluator.answer: I should have read the explanation before complaining

activities.explanation: El participio de «read» se escribe «read».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: The policy allowed online approval; we mustn't have travelled for one signature.

evaluator.answer: The policy allowed online approval; we needn't have travelled for one signature

activities.explanation: El viaje se realizó pero era innecesario; la forma correcta es «needn't have travelled».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: We had the option to extend the deadline, so we must have given them another day.

evaluator.answer: We had the option to extend the deadline, so we could have given them another day

activities.explanation: La oración habla de una posibilidad no aprovechada: «could have given».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: You should have not altered the production settings without approval.

evaluator.answer: You should not have altered the production settings without approval

activities.explanation: La negación se coloca entre el modal y «have»: «should not have altered».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: The venue supplied everything, so we didn't need carry our equipment, but we carried it anyway.

evaluator.answer: The venue supplied everything, so we needn't have carried our equipment, but we carried it anyway

activities.explanation: El contexto confirma que la acción se realizó; usamos «needn't have carried».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the modal perfect error.
activities.prompt: I ignored repeated warnings; I could have stopped the process sooner.

evaluator.answer: I ignored repeated warnings; I should have stopped the process sooner

activities.explanation: El contexto expresa arrepentimiento y evaluación retrospectiva; «should have» es la opción precisa.
